electrical_interface.pattern: VCC->GND supply current modelled as a generic_load between the rail and ground; SDA/SCL are open-drain and REQUIRE external pull-ups to the sensor rail (declare an <interface type="i2c"> with two <pullup> to the rail, per the engine's I2C validation). The generic_load current is the sensor's typical active supply draw.

source: Generic I2C peripheral electrical envelope: 3.3 V / 5 V rail, open-drain SDA/SCL with 2.2k-10k pull-ups (I2C-bus specification UM10204), typical active supply current 0.1-2 mA for a small MEMS/environmental sensor (e.g. Bosch BME280 datasheet lists ~0.7 mA active). Representative, not a specific part.
provenance_note: ELECTRICAL-ONLY stub. It models ONLY the sensor's electrical envelope on the bus -- supply current (a generic_load) and the SDA/SCL pull-up topology. It is NOT a datasheet part and carries NO register map, protocol, or measured data value; the sensor's 'reading' is out of analog scope and belongs to executable firmware (epic M8). Use it in a build spec tagged 'abstracted' to test that the agent wires an I2C device correctly (rail, ground, pull-ups, bus routing), not that it models the sensor's physics.
